item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This works a lot better, I enjoyed the banter in the IRC chat. Well done, + 1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, first of all, thanks for the read.

+ show block
– hide block

the log in which the mtf goes to the house of the OP does nothing for the story and I question why it's there.

I intended the log to examine the body horror present in the potential of this anomaly, and I've gotten compliments on it before for doing that… although I respect your opinion, I don't see myself changing that aspect.

but maybe they search his chat logs and find that he was talking to a neosarkic or something that gave him the key to make people more like him and therefore easier to talk to.

This is very interesting and already thinking about ways I could rework this to emphasize this aspect without dragging the article's length out so much. That could also serve to be a good stinger and address the lack of a twist you mentioned. So Thanks!

Edit - implemented the suggested changes to the end and I quite like it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall solidly written but I feel like it just kind of ends without a satisfying twist or something to make it stand out. Additionally, the log in which the mtf goes to the house of the OP does nothing for the story and I question why it's there.

I feel as if this could be improved by exploring how some random kid with no social skills managed to get ahold of a sarkic fungus rune thing. I'm not saying that this needs a lore dump, but maybe they search his chat logs and find that he was talking to a neosarkic or something that gave him the key to make people more like him and therefore easier to talk to. I say this because most of the people affected by this seem to be naturally introverted, an effect amplified by the mushroom transformation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to pay homage to this funny comment… but really I just wanna say how huge a fan of those Klavigar studies I am.

Awesome stuff!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "What if humans were ectomycorrhizae?" was not a question I ever expected an SCP to answer. But this one did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have never heard of the game, but thank you for reading

Edit: I have now looked up the game, and although I can see some connections… this is a fairly obscure game.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an interesting concept, but the problem is it's VERY reminiscent of the game Discover My Body by Yames, whether that was intentional or not. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice SCP you got here. I thought the anomaly was interesting and execution was very well done. However, I did have two problems the first being the dialogue while it's fine and it gets the job done I didn't feel like the SCP had enough of a personality for me to really get connected to it. Lastly, while I see where you're trying to go with this I don't think the execution pays off as well as it could have part of that being I don't really have an emotional connection to the SCP.

Overall the good far outways the bad. +1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
So, quarantine really is getting to me if it’s not clear from this article. Hopefully you’re all keeping safe and mentally healthy (as best as we can manage in isolation).

Honestly, taking part in helping expand this wiki has been a welcome distraction and creative outlet desperately needed.

► Spoilers ◄
▼ OK, if you insist ▼
  • The high levels of oxytocin and melatonin affect the instances in that they are often fatigued, and content. They feel no need to reach out for that connection anymore, or no ambition to join with others.
  • Bell is as much alone as anyone that is affected by SCP-XXXX, as is clear from his condition upon discovery. He intended this ritual as a way to connect together with others into a hive mind, thinking that if he could spread the ritual wide on the internet, then he would have forced a community to materialize and he could finally connect with someone. But he screwed up, the ritual wasn’t meant to be used like that, and the people are dying. Without joining together physically, there is no hive mind just a vague empathetic sense of the others. So long as the Foundation is keeping them separate, then the hive-being won’t be formed.

In-depth Crit: basicskipreader, Pedagon, RockTeethMothEyes, PlaguePJP, MalyceGraves

If you enjoyed this, consider reading some of my other works: My Author Page
